title: Boat Rental in Guadeloupe: A 2026 Guide to the Best Anchorages and Costs
topic: things to do by boat in Guadeloupe
html:
Guadeloupe, a butterfly-shaped archipelago in the Caribbean, offers some of the finest boating in the region. Whether you depart from Marina Bas du Fort in Pointe-à-Pitre or Marina de Saint-François, you can expect to spend from €250 for a half-day motorboat to €3,500 for a crewed catamaran. On BoatHire24, you can compare verified listings and book your ideal vessel.

Where to set off in Guadeloupe

Guadeloupe is shaped like a butterfly, with Grande-Terre and Basse-Terre separated by a narrow channel. The main departure points include Marina Bas du Fort in Pointe-à-Pitre, which is the largest marina on the island and offers full services. Marina de Saint-François on the south-east coast is a gateway to the Îles des Saintes and Marie-Galante. Other options are Marina de Rivière Sens in Gourbeyre for southern Basse-Terre, and the port of Deshaies in the north-west, near the famous Cousteau Reserve. For those seeking the eastern coast, the anchorage at Le Moule provides a quieter alternative. You can hire a boat in a few minutes.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Guadeloupe?

Costs vary by boat type and season, starting at €80 per person for a shared tour and reaching €5,000 for a luxury crewed yacht per day. A half-day rental of a small motorboat (up to 6 passengers) typically ranges from €250 to €400, while a full-day catamaran charter for up to 12 people costs between €1,200 and €2,500. Crewed yachts with skipper and host can cost €3,500 to €5,000 per day. To get the best value, you can hire a boat on BoatHire24, where you can compare prices and read reviews before booking. One option worth a look is the Azimut 58 for hire.

  • Shared tour (half-day): €80, €120 per person
  • Small motorboat (half-day, up to 6 guests): €250, €400
  • Catamaran (full-day, up to 12 guests): €1,200, €2,500
  • Crewed yacht (full-day, up to 8 guests): €3,500, €5,000

Which boat to rent

Your choice of boat depends on your group size and itinerary. Below are the most popular options for Guadeloupe. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

  1. Motorboat (open console), ideal for day trips to nearby islands such as Îles des Saintes, carrying up to 6 guests, from €300 per day.
  2. Catamaran, spacious and stable, perfect for families or groups of up to 12, from €1,500 per day.
  3. Sailing yacht, for a traditional experience, often with a skipper, carrying up to 8 guests, from €800 per day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht, such as the Azimut 58 for hire, offering comfort and speed for up to 10 guests, from €2,500 per day. See the Azimut 58 listing for full specifications.
  5. RIB (rigid inflatable boat), fast and maneuverable, great for watersports and short hops, carrying up to 6 guests, from €200 per half-day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Guadeloupe?

Renting a bareboat powerboat under 20hp does not require a licence in French waters, but larger vessels often require a valid licence or a skipper charter. Many rental operators offer the option to hire a skipper for an additional fee, which removes the licence requirement. For sailing yachts and larger powerboats, a recognised International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or national equivalent is typically needed. The operator will confirm exact requirements at booking, so always check before you reserve. To understand the booking process fully, see how boat rental works on BoatHire24. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

Local tip: The predominant trade wind in Guadeloupe is the Alizé, blowing from the east-north-east. High season runs from December to April, when seas are calmest and visibility is best for exploring the Cousteau Reserve.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80, €5,000 per day
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours) to full-day (8 hours) or longer
  • Capacity: 2 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Guadeloupe

Can I rent a boat in Guadeloupe without a licence?

Yes, you can rent a small motorboat under 20hp without a licence, or you can charter a boat with a skipper included.

What is the best month to rent a boat in Guadeloupe?

The best months are from December to April, during the dry season and when the Alizé winds provide steady sailing conditions.

How far is it to Îles des Saintes from Guadeloupe?

The distance from Saint-François to the Îles des Saintes is approximately 20 nautical miles, a one-hour crossing by motorboat.

Is it safe to rent a boat in Guadeloupe?

Yes, provided you choose a reputable operator, check weather conditions, and follow local navigation rules; the main risks are coral reefs and sudden squalls.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned sailor, renting a boat in Guadeloupe opens up a world of secluded beaches and vibrant marine life. BoatHire24 makes it easy to compare options and book with confidence. If you own a boat in Guadeloupe, you can list your boat on BoatHire24 and start earning from the thriving tourism market.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Guadeloupe and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
